speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: So smile the heavens upon this holy act

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: That after-hours with sorrow chide us not.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Amen, amen. But come what sorrow can,

speaker: ROMEO
text: It cannot countervail the exchange of joy

speaker: ROMEO
text: That one short minute gives me in her sight.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Do thou but close our hands with holy words,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Then love-devouring death do what he dare,

speaker: ROMEO
text: It is enough I may but call her mine.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: These violent delights have violent ends

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And in their triumph die, like fire and powder,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Which, as they kiss, consume. The sweetest honey

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Is loathsome in his own deliciousness

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And in the taste confounds the appetite.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Therefore love moderately. Long love doth so.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Too swift arrives as tardy as too slow.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Here comes the lady. O, so light a foot

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Will ne’er wear out the everlasting flint.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: A lover may bestride the gossamers

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: That idles in the wanton summer air,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And yet not fall, so light is vanity.

speaker: JULIET
text: Good even to my ghostly confessor.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Romeo shall thank thee, daughter, for us both.

speaker: JULIET
text: As much to him, else is his thanks too much.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ah, Juliet, if the measure of thy joy

speaker: ROMEO
text: Be heaped like mine, and that thy skill be more

speaker: ROMEO
text: To blazon it, then sweeten with thy breath

speaker: ROMEO
text: This neighbor air, and let rich music’s tongue

speaker: ROMEO
text: Unfold the imagined happiness that both

speaker: ROMEO
text: Receive in either by this dear encounter.

speaker: JULIET
text: Conceit, more rich in matter than in words,

speaker: JULIET
text: Brags of his substance, not of ornament.

speaker: JULIET
text: They are but beggars that can count their worth,

speaker: JULIET
text: But my true love is grown to such excess

speaker: JULIET
text: I cannot sum up sum of half my wealth.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Come, come with me, and we will make short work,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For, by your leaves, you shall not stay alone

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Till Holy Church incorporate two in one.
